channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flytipping, 20th March
item.description:
There has been alot of bags of clothing, toys and a chair left outside the community hospice shop, all the bags are split open and clothing spread everywhere, along with the toys, they cannot be used and it is a health and safety issue, please can everything that is outside the shop be removed as a matter or urgency.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter left by council blowing on street, 20th March
item.description:
Huge mess on Queensbridge Rd where council have left rubbish bags and rubbish all over street
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensbridge Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ardmere Road - no street sweeping, 20th March
item.description:
We used to get the street swept every week. Now it doesn’t get swept at all. I’d like to know if our road has been accidentally left off a route or something? Thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ardmere Road, Hither Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins continually blocking pavement, 20th March
item.description:
Householder storing bins outside garden on the pavement (even though has huge back garden) making it awkward for wheelchair and push chairs. Asked to move bins into garden as per other household but refuses. Haringey need to write to their housing association which is Clarion. To tell their tenant to abide by local rules. Note residents have tried so needs to come from higher authority like the council as they own the pavement. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beaconsfield Road, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped car no number plates, 20th March
item.description:
This car has been dumped here for over a month. Unable to repoty to dvla due to number plates removed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riverdale Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Christchurch x Brondesbury park, 20th March
item.description:
Bags, hoover

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Park, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Rubbish dumped, 20th March
item.description:
Gas cylinders on verge on Wimborne ave near roundabout. Wood discarded on verge at the bottom of Berens Way near roundabout. Bucket and paint discarded on verge on Marlings park ave near junction with Orpington rd. Another gas cylinder nearby
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Ravensbury Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Refuse bags on street, 20th March
item.description:
Multiple refuse bags with food waste and household rubbish left on street. All left unpacked up by refuse collectors on Friday morning. This is a regular problem.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gloucester Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Contractor damaged pavement, 20th March
item.description:
156 Green Dragon Lane. This pavement and surrounding footpath/verge has been damaged by the contractor and nothing has been done to repair for 6 monhths or more. Paving is uneven, loose and broken.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Dragon Lane, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 blabk sacks dumped, 20th March
item.description:
Next to large oak tree is a bush with red berries. Someone has put 2 black sacks underneath here and flies are around it. Tried to add photo but it won't upload
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Arbroath Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter at and behind bus stop, 20th March
item.description:
In this what3words location ///risk.dock.parts behind and at the bus stop and bush at the left of the bus stop there is litter everywhere
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Anerley Hill, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped Pallets, 20th March
item.description:
2dumped pallets & leaves shopping basket in corner

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31-33, High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hackney council waste bags have not been collected for weeks, 20th March
item.description:
These bags have been left uncollected for a couple of weeks now and the spot is being used as a rubbish bin. Please arrange collection

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pownall Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cans and other litter in bush behind bench, 20th March
item.description:
In this what3words location:///issued.lake.hurray Litter everywhere in this area, at the bus stop, in the bushes. One of the litter bins on station road should be moved either near the bench or near the bus stop. There are currently two bins one near the other on station road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 33, Anerley Hill, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Calthorpe street between Gough street and Gray's Inn road, 20th March
item.description:
Multiple potholes for many months in Calthorpe Street - it's only a matter of time before a cyclist gets killed swerving to avoid one and getting hit my a car. Camden will be responsible for corporate manslaughter is this happens.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Calthorpe Street, Holborn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bike covered in bird poo left directly outside waitrose, 20th March
item.description:
Has been there since 7pm or so last night - more than 12 hours

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Parade, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Footpath restricted barriers - overgrown hedge, 20th March
item.description:
In the footpath between Swanton Road and Sussex Road, by the restricted barrier near Sussex Road, it is very difficult to pass through with a powerchair because of the overgrown hedge (images 2 and 3). The entry at Swanton Road is perfect (image 1).

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sussex Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Please remove from Datchelor Place, 20th March
item.description:
Day 2

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 55, Camberwell Church Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Child nearly hit on Horse Guards Road because live road looks pedestrian, 20th March
item.description:
I would like to report a dangerous and misleading section of Horse Guards Road. The section of road shown in the attached photo (please disregard the map marker, as the system did not allow me to select the exact spot) is surfaced in brown rather than the standard grey used for roads and has no visible road markings. Because of this, it can very easily be mistaken for pedestrian space, especially by people coming from St James’s Park towards Horse Guards Parade, which also has a brown surface. The visual transition makes it look as though the pedestrian area simply continues, rather than clearly indicating that this is still a live road for vehicles. The layout is made more dangerous by the corners, which limit visibility. When crossing, pedestrians cannot properly see cars approaching until they are already very close. On 19 March, sometime between 17:30 and 17:45, there was a near accident at this location. A young child rode through the road on his bike when the road appeared completely empty and there was no visible sign of approaching traffic. Two black cabs then suddenly came around the corner at speed. The parent following the child believed the area was pedestrian because of how it looks, and so was following behind rather than holding the child’s hand and being on full alert for live traffic. This created a near miss that could easily have ended in a serious accident. This risk appears to come directly from the road design and lack of clear visual warning that vehicles use this space. Please could this location be urgently reviewed for clearer signage, road markings, surface differentiation, visibility improvements, or other safety measures to prevent a serious accident.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Mall, Victoria, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: South Kilburn Open Space, Malvern Road side, all along the wall by Perrin House, 20th March
item.description:
There is litter all the way along the wall in the park bordering Perrin House. It looks awful, please clear it and include the area by the wall in future litter clearance. Regular emptying of the bins and litter clearance has got a lot better over the past year but for some reason this area isn't being included.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malvern Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
